assumptions: the first commercial release should focus on one hero product
the first user journey should prioritize low friction and fast checkout
scan prompts should stay short, visual, and easy to retry
recommendation should remain single-path in the first release
risks: too many choices may reduce conversion
weak scan quality may increase drop-off
unclear microcopy may confuse first-time users
forced account creation may reduce completion rate
next_required_validation: Hermes review before wireframe implementation and UI build
primary_user_goal: Help a first-time user understand the product quickly, complete a guided foot scan, receive a simple recommendation, and finish checkout with minimal friction.
ux_recommendation: Implement the first ZILFIT mobile journey as a guided, one-product, one-path flow with clear scan prompts, one primary CTA per screen, visible trust cues, and the shortest possible route to checkout.
friction_points: too many product choices too early
success_standard: user reaches checkout with minimal friction
next_design_step: Convert this wireframe spec into implementation-ready screen wireframes, CTA microcopy, and state logic for the ZILFIT mobile app.
